salary-cap.plain_definition: The maximum amount each team can spend on player salaries in a season. The NFL cap (2025) is around $255M. The NBA cap is around $141M with a luxury tax kicking in over $171M.

lens_flavors.corporate-girlie: Annual headcount budget. Your CFO (the GM) approves it. Going over = quarterly review with finance.

free-agency.plain_definition: When a player's contract ends, they can sign with any team that wants them. NFL free agency starts in mid-March. NBA free agency starts June 30.

lens_flavors.bachelor: Free agent meetings are private one-on-ones. The contract signing is the final rose.

trade-deadline.plain_definition: The last day teams can trade players that season. NFL: early November. NBA: early February. After the deadline, rosters are mostly locked for the playoff run.

draft.plain_definition: The annual event where teams pick new players from college (NBA) or college + sometimes other levels (NFL). Worst teams pick first (with a lottery in the NBA to discourage tanking).

luxury-tax.plain_definition: If your NBA team payroll goes over the cap by enough, you pay a tax to the league (which gets redistributed to teams that didn't go over). The new 'second apron' (2024 CBA) makes going way over very painful.

lens_flavors.corporate-girlie: Q4 overage that legal makes you eat. The CFO's least favorite line item.

buyout.plain_definition: When a player and a team mutually agree to end the contract early. The team eats some money, the player becomes a free agent and usually signs with a contender.

lens_flavors.bachelor: The 'I came here for the wrong reasons' confessional, then production drives them to the airport.

max-contract.plain_definition: The biggest contract a team can offer a player. Tiered by years of NBA experience: ~25%/30%/35% of the cap depending on tenure. The 'supermax' is the biggest deal possible (5 years, 35%) — only available to a player's current team if they hit certain awards.

lens_flavors.bachelor: The final rose, but with $300M on the back.
lens_flavors.corporate-girlie: Your VP-level offer with a 5-year vesting cliff.

franchise-tag.plain_definition: A 1-year contract teams can force on a player to keep them for one more season instead of letting them hit free agency. Pays the average of the top-5 salaries at that position. Players hate it (no long-term security), teams use it as leverage.

rookie-scale.plain_definition: First-round NBA picks sign 4-year contracts at predetermined amounts based on draft slot. Cheap labor for 4 years before they can negotiate a real deal. The reason teams love top picks.

no-trade-clause.plain_definition: A contract clause where a player has to approve any trade involving them. Very rare in the NBA — only LeBron James has a true no-trade clause currently (only players with 8+ years NBA experience and 4+ with their current team can get one).

supermax.plain_definition: The biggest contract in the NBA — 5 years, 35% of the cap. Only available to your current team and only if you've made All-NBA, MVP, or DPOY recently. Examples: Jokic, SGA, Tatum.

lens_flavors.bachelor: Final rose AND the family ring.

two-way-contract.plain_definition: A contract for fringe NBA players — they split time between the NBA team and its G League affiliate. Limited NBA games allowed per season. Teams get 3 two-way slots.

tampering.plain_definition: Talking to a player (or his agent) while he's still under contract with another team. League rule. Everyone does it. Almost nobody gets caught.

lens_flavors.bachelor: Meeting another contestant's brother for coffee 'just to talk.'

first-round-pick.plain_definition: The most-valuable trade asset that isn't a player. Teams package + protect + delay them. NFL has 7 rounds, NBA has 2. First-round picks usually become starters.

g-league.plain_definition: The NBA's developmental league. Where two-way players, fringe NBA guys, and prospects play. Each NBA team has an affiliate.

practice-squad.plain_definition: A 16-player squad that practices with the NFL team but isn't on the active roster on game day. Usually young guys, projects, or veterans waiting for an injury opening.

rebuild.plain_definition: When a team trades away veterans for young players + draft picks because they're not winning anyway. Usually takes 2-4 years. The OKC Thunder (post-Westbrook) is the gold standard.

lens_flavors.swiftie: Folklore-coded. They went into the woods. They'll come back as Speak Now.

tank.plain_definition: Losing on purpose (or making it really easy to lose) to get a higher draft pick. The NBA's draft lottery exists to discourage it. Doesn't always work — see the 76ers' 'Process' era.

playoff-bracket.plain_definition: The tournament chart that decides who plays who in the postseason. Top seeds get easier first-round matchups. Win 4 series in NBA, win 3 in NFL, you're in the championship.

mvp.plain_definition: Regular-season award given to the player voted most valuable by sportswriters/broadcasters. NBA's recent winners: Jokic (3x), Embiid, Giannis (2x). NFL's recent: Mahomes (3x), Allen, Lamar (2x).

all-pro-vs-pro-bowl.plain_definition: Pro Bowl is a popularity contest (fan votes count) — making it is nice but devalued. All-Pro (1st-team / 2nd-team) is voted by 50 sportswriters and is the real best-at-position award. All-Pro = elite.

all-nba.plain_definition: Three teams (1st, 2nd, 3rd) of 15 total players voted as the league's best. Making All-NBA unlocks supermax eligibility — so it's both an honor and a contract trigger.

lens_flavors.corporate-girlie: Performance bonus tier — All-NBA is the unlock for stock.

play-in-tournament.plain_definition: Mini-tournament for seeds 7-10 in each conference to determine the final two playoff spots. Created in 2020. Reduces tanking incentive at the bottom.

wild-card.plain_definition: Playoff spots given to non-division-winners with the best records. Each NFL conference has 3 wild cards. The opening playoff weekend is called Wild Card Weekend.
